docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:256:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:258:## Design Decisions (session 4)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:260:### New apparatus: The Instrumentarium (`tools`)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:262:**Problem:** Tools are currently owned by Arbor (`listTools()`, `findTool()`), but Arbor's design goal is "plugin loader only." Tools need a home that both the session layer (Loom/Animator) and the CLI can depend on, without coupling either to anima identity.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:264:**Decision:** Create a new apparatus — **The Instrumentarium** (plugin id `tools`, package `@shardworks/tools-apparatus`). It owns:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:265:- Tool registry — scanning kit `tools` contributions and apparatus `supportKit` tools at startup
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:266:- Role-gating resolution — given a set of roles + baseTools, return the resolved tool set
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:267:- CLI tool discovery — `nsg <tool>` resolves through The Instrumentarium
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:269:The Instrumentarium has no dependency on animas, sessions, or composition. Both The Loom and the CLI depend on it independently. Apparatus that need to invoke tools programmatically depend on it.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:271:`consumes: ["tools"]` — scans kit and supportKit contributions for tool definitions.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:273:### Loom MVP — composition without identity
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:275:**Problem:** Full anima composition (identity lookup → curriculum resolution → temperament resolution → charter + tool instructions) requires several systems that don't exist yet. But The Animator needs *some* composed context to launch sessions.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:277:**Decision:** MVP Loom is a pass-through — the caller provides the system prompt and optional initial prompt. The Loom packages them into a `WovenContext` that The Animator consumes. No role resolution, no tool instructions, no file reading, no identity lookup.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:279:The Loom exists as a separate apparatus even at MVP so that The Animator never assembles prompts itself. As composition grows (role instructions, tool instructions, curricula, temperaments, charter), The Loom's internals change but its output shape (`WovenContext`) stays stable — The Animator is unaffected.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:281:### Animator MVP
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:283:**Decision:** MVP Animator takes a `WovenContext` (from Loom) + a working directory and:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:284:1. Launches a session provider (e.g. `claude-code-apparatus`) with the system prompt
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:285:2. Monitors the process
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:286:3. Records the session result to The Stacks (sessions book)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:288:No MCP tool server, no Instrumentarium dependency, no role awareness in MVP. Tool-equipped sessions with MCP are documented as future state in `apparatus/animator.md`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:290:### Dependency graph (MVP)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:292:```
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:293:The Stacks (books)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:294:    │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:295:    └── The Animator (animator)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:296:            │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:297:            └── The Loom (loom)   ← zero apparatus dependencies, pass-through
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:299:The Clockworks (clockworks)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:300:    │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:301:    └── summon relay → The Loom → The Animator
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:303:The Instrumentarium (tools)   ← no dependencies in MVP, not yet wired to sessions
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:304:    │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:305:    └── CLI (nsg)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:306:```
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:308:Note: in MVP, The Loom and The Animator do not depend on The Instrumentarium. Tool-equipped sessions (Animator → Instrumentarium for MCP tool set) are future state.

1	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:1:# The Review Loop — Design Spec
2	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:3:Status: **Design** (not yet implemented)
3	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:5:> **Not a traditional apparatus.** The review loop does not have a `start()`/`stop()` lifecycle or a persistent runtime API. It is a composition pattern — a pair of engine designs and a rig structure — within the rigging system. This document specifies the design as implemented in the Spider.
4	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:7:---
5	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:9:## Purpose
6	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:11:The review loop moves quality assurance inside the rig. Instead of dispatching a commission once and surfacing the result to the patron regardless of quality, the rig runs an implementation pass, evaluates the result against concrete criteria, and — if the criteria are not met — runs a revision pass. The patron receives work only after it has cleared at least one automated review gate, or after the loop has exhausted its retry budget.
7	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:13:This is not a general-purpose test harness. The review loop does one thing: catch the most common and cheapest-to-detect failure modes before they become patron problems.
8	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:15:**What the review loop is not:**
9	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:16:- A replacement for spec quality. A bad spec produces bad work; the review loop helps only when the anima had the information to succeed but failed in execution.
10	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:17:- A Clockworks-dependent system. The loop runs entirely within the rigging pipeline using existing apparatus.
11	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:18:- A complete quality gate. The MVP catches mechanical failures; richer review criteria are future scope.
12	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:20:---
13	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:22:## Empirical Motivation
14	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:24:Commission log X013 (`experiments/data/commission-log.yaml`) through 2026-04-02 shows the following outcome distribution across patron-tracked commissions with known outcomes:
15	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:26:| Outcome | Count | Notes |
16	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:27:|---------|-------|-------|
17	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:28:| success | 7 | Includes 1 with revision_required=true (partial attribution issue) |
18	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:29:| partial | 2 | Required follow-up commissions |
19	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:30:| abandoned | 3 | Two were test/infra noise; one was execution_error |
20	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:31:| cancelled | 1 | Process failure, not work failure |
21	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:33:Of the real work failures, the two most common causes were:
22	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:34:1. **Uncommitted changes** — anima produced correct work but did not commit before session end. Mechanically detectable.
23	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:35:2. **Partial execution** — anima completed some of the spec but missed a subsystem (e.g. missed a test file, broke a build). Partially detectable via build/test runs.
24	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:37:Both are catchable with cheap, mechanical review criteria. Neither requires an LLM judge. This is the MVP's target.
25	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:39:---
26	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:41:## Design Decision: Where Does the Loop Live?
27	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:43:Three candidate locations were considered:
28	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:45:### Option B: Review engine in every rig (full design)
29	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:47:The Spider seeds every rig with an `implement → review → [revise → review]*N` chain by default. The review engine is a clockwork engine; the revise engine is a quick engine. Both are standard engine designs contributed by a kit.
30	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:49:**Pros:** Architecturally clean. Composes naturally with Spider's traversal. Reusable engine designs. Composes naturally with the Spider — the rig handles iteration natively.
31	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:51:**Cons:** Requires the Spider. Not implementable until the rigging system exists.
32	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:53:### Option C: Rig pattern via origination engine
33	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:55:The origination engine seeds rigs with review chains by default. Superficially similar to Option B, but the decision of whether to include a review loop is made at origination time, not by a default rig structure.
34	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:57:**Pros:** Gives origination agency over review strategy (some work may not need review; some may need richer review).
35	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:59:**Cons:** Complicates origination. Review is almost always appropriate; making it opt-in inverts the sensible default.
36	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:61:### Decision
37	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:63:**Option B (review engines in the rig) is the chosen design.**
38	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:65:The Spider seeds every rig with an `implement → review → revise → seal` chain. The review engine is a clockwork engine that runs mechanical checks and a reviewer session; the revise engine is a quick engine. Both are standard engine designs contributed by the Spider's support kit. The rig pattern (Option C) governs per-commission review configuration as a future enhancement.
39	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:67:---

42	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:73:#### `review` engine (clockwork)
43	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:75:**Design:**
44	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:76:```typescript
45	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:77:{
46	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:78:  id: 'review',
47	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:79:  kind: 'clockwork',
48	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:80:  inputs: ['writId', 'worktreePath', 'attempt'],
49	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:81:  outputs: ['reviewResult'],
50	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:82:  config: {
51	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:83:    checks: ['uncommitted_changes', 'build', 'test'],
52	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:84:    buildCommand: string | undefined,
53	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:85:    testCommand: string | undefined,
54	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:86:  }
55	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:87:}
56	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:88:```
57	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:90:The review engine runs the same three checks as the MVP. It writes a `ReviewResult` to its yield. It does not branch — it always completes, passing the result downstream.
58	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:92:The downstream engine (either a `seal` engine or a `revise` engine) reads `reviewResult.passed` to decide what to do. The Spider sees a completed engine regardless of outcome; the branching logic lives in the rig structure (see Rig Pattern below).
59	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:94:#### `revise` engine (quick)
60	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:96:**Design:**
61	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:97:```typescript
62	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:98:{
63	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:99:  id: 'revise',
64	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:100:  kind: 'quick',
65	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:101:  inputs: ['writId', 'worktreePath', 'reviewResult', 'attempt'],
66	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:102:  outputs: ['sessionResult'],
67	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:103:  role: 'artificer',
68	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:104:}
69	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:105:```
70	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:107:The revise engine assembles the revision prompt (same template as MVP) and launches an anima session. The session runs in the existing worktree — it does not open a new draft.
71	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:109:### Rig Pattern
72	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:111:The default rig for a commission with review enabled:
73	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:113:```
74	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:114:                ┌──────────────┐
75	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:115:                │  implement   │  (quick engine: artificer)
76	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:116:                │    engine    │
77	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:117:                └──────┬───────┘
78	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:118:                       │ yield: sessionResult
79	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:119:                       ▼
80	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:120:                ┌──────────────┐
81	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:121:                │    review    │  (clockwork engine)
82	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:122:                │   engine 1  │
83	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:123:                └──────┬───────┘
84	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:124:                       │ yield: reviewResult
85	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:125:          ┌────────────┴────────────┐
86	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:126:          │ passed                  │ failed (attempt < maxRetries)
87	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:127:          ▼                         ▼
88	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:128:   ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐
89	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:129:   │    seal     │         │     revise       │  (quick engine: artificer)
90	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:130:   │   engine    │         │     engine 1     │
91	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:131:   └─────────────┘         └────────┬─────────┘
92	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:132:                                    │ yield: sessionResult
93	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:133:                                    ▼
94	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:134:                           ┌──────────────────┐
95	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:135:                           │     review       │  (clockwork engine)
96	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:136:                           │    engine 2      │
97	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:137:                           └────────┬─────────┘
98	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:138:                                    │ yield: reviewResult
99	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:139:                       ┌────────────┴────────────┐
100	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:140:                       │ passed                  │ failed
101	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:141:                       ▼                         ▼
102	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:142:                ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐
103	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:143:                │    seal     │         │    escalate      │  (clockwork engine)
104	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:144:                │   engine    │         │    engine        │
105	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:145:                └─────────────┘         └──────────────────┘
106	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:146:```
107	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:148:The Spider traverses this graph naturally. Each engine completes and propagates its yield; downstream engines activate when their upstream is complete. The conditional branching (pass → seal, fail → revise) is expressed in the rig structure, not in Spider logic — the Spider just runs whatever is ready.
108	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:150:**Seeding the rig:** The origination engine produces this graph when it seeds the rig. For `maxRetries=2`, the origination engine seeds a fixed graph (not dynamically extended). If the guild wants `maxRetries=0` (no review loop), origination seeds the simple `implement → seal` graph.
109	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:152:**Dynamic extension (future):** A more sophisticated design would have the review engine declare a `need: 'revision'` when it fails, and the Fabricator would resolve and graft the next revise+review pair. This avoids pre-seeding the full graph and enables arbitrary retry depths. This is Future scope — the fixed graph is sufficient for MVP and avoids Spider complexity in the initial rigging implementation.
110	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:154:### Spider Integration
111	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:156:The Spider needs no changes to support the review loop. It already:
112	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:157:- Traverses all engines whose upstream is complete
113	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:158:- Routes ready engines to the Executor
114	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:159:- Handles both clockwork and quick engine kinds
115	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:161:The review loop is just a graph shape that Spider happens to traverse. The `escalate` clockwork engine signals the Clerk with a `failed` transition; the `seal` clockwork engine signals completion. The Spider itself is agnostic.
116	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:163:---
117	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:165:## Review Criteria Reference
118	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:167:### MVP Criteria (Mechanical)
119	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:169:| Check | Description | Detection Method | Cost |
120	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:170:|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
121	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:171:| `uncommitted_changes` | All work is committed | `git status --porcelain` | < 100ms |
122	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:172:| `build` | Build command exits cleanly | Run configured build command | Varies |
123	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:173:| `test` | Test suite passes | Run configured test command | Varies |
124	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:175:The `uncommitted_changes` check is always enabled. Build and test checks are opt-in via guild configuration.
125	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:177:### Future Criteria (Judgment-Required)
126	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:179:These are not in scope for MVP but are the natural next layer:
127	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:181:| Check | Description | Detection Method | Cost |
128	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:182:|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
129	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:183:| `spec_coverage` | Diff addresses spec requirements | LLM-as-judge pass on (spec, diff) | Medium |
130	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:184:| `no_regressions` | No tests were deleted or disabled | Diff analysis | Low |
131	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:185:| `type_check` | TypeScript compilation passes | `tsc --noEmit` | Varies |
132	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:186:| `lint` | Linter passes | Run configured lint command | Varies |
133	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:188:The LLM-as-judge `spec_coverage` check is the most valuable future criterion — it catches the "anima only addressed part of the spec" failure mode that mechanical checks miss. It requires a separate quick engine with access to the writ body and the diff, and a structured prompt asking whether the diff achieves the spec's stated goals.
134	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:190:---
135	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:192:## Artifact Schema
136	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:194:Every review pass writes an artifact. Artifacts live in the commission data directory alongside the existing artifacts written by the Laboratory.
137	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:196:### Location
138	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:198:```
139	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:199:experiments/data/commissions/<writ-id>/
140	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:200:  commission.md          (existing — writ body)
141	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:201:  review.md              (existing template — patron review slot)
142	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:202:  review-loop/
143	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:203:    attempt-1/
144	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:204:      review.md          (ReviewResult as structured markdown)
145	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:205:      git-status.txt     (git status output)
146	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:206:      git-diff.txt       (git diff HEAD output)
147	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:207:    attempt-2/
148	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:208:      review.md
149	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:209:      git-status.txt
150	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:210:      git-diff.txt
151	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:211:    escalation.md        (if loop exhausted; patron-facing summary)
152	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:212:```
153	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:214:The review engine writes these artifacts via the Stacks or directly to the commission data directory.
154	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:216:### `review.md` Schema
155	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:218:```markdown
156	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:219:# Review — Attempt {N}
157	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:221:**Writ:** {writId}
158	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:222:**Timestamp:** {ISO 8601}
159	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:223:**Result:** PASSED | FAILED
160	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:225:## Checks
161	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:227:| Check | Result | Duration |
162	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:228:|-------|--------|----------|
163	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:229:| uncommitted_changes | ✓ PASS / ✗ FAIL | {ms}ms |
164	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:230:| build | ✓ PASS / ✗ FAIL | {ms}ms |
165	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:231:| test | ✓ PASS / ✗ FAIL | {ms}ms |
166	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:233:## Failures
167	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:235:{for each failure}
168	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:236:### {check}
169	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:237:{message}
170	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:239:```
171	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:240:{detail}
172	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:241:```
173	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:242:{end for}
174	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:243:```
175	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:245:### `escalation.md` Schema
176	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:247:```markdown
177	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:248:# Review Loop Escalated
178	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:250:**Writ:** {writId}
179	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:251:**Title:** {writ.title}
180	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:252:**Attempts:** {N}
181	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:253:**Timestamp:** {ISO 8601}
182	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:255:The review loop exhausted its retry budget ({maxRetries} retries) without
183	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:256:achieving a passing review. The draft has been abandoned.
184	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:258:## Summary of Failures
185	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:260:{for each attempt}
186	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:261:### Attempt {N}
187	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:262:{list of failed checks with messages}
188	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:263:{end for}
189	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:265:## Recommended Actions
190	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:267:- Inspect the worktree state preserved in the draft artifacts
191	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:268:- Review the git-diff.txt files in each attempt directory
192	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:269:- Revise the spec to address the observed failure mode before re-dispatching
193	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:270:```
194	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:272:---
195	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:274:## Configuration
196	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:276:Review configuration lives in `guild.json`:
197	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:278:```json
198	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:279:{
199	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:280:  "review": {
200	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:281:    "enabled": true,
201	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:282:    "maxRetries": 2,
202	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:283:    "buildCommand": "pnpm build",
203	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:284:    "testCommand": "pnpm test"
204	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:285:  }
205	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:286:}
206	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:287:```
207	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:289:All fields are optional. `enabled` defaults to `false` (opt-in). The intent is to make it default-on once the loop has been validated in practice. This configuration is consumed by the origination engine to decide whether to seed the review graph and what configuration to pass to the review engine.
208	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:291:---
209	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:293:## Observability
210	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:295:The review loop is itself experiment data. Every iteration produces artifacts that the Laboratory can capture and analyze:
211	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:297:1. **Review artifacts** (`review-loop/attempt-N/`) — structured pass/fail evidence for each check. Enables quantitative analysis: which checks catch what failure modes? How often does the second attempt pass where the first failed?
212	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:299:2. **Session records** — revision sessions are recorded in the Animator's `sessions` book with `metadata.trigger: 'review-revision'` and `metadata.attempt: N`. Enables cost accounting: how much does the review loop add per commission?
213	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:301:3. **Writ resolution field** — when the loop escalates, the writ resolution includes the retry count. The commission log's `failure_mode` can be set to `review_exhausted` to distinguish review-loop failures from first-try failures.
214	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:303:4. **Commission log** — the `revision_required` field will more accurately reflect anima-driven revisions vs. patron-driven revisions once the review loop is active. The distinction becomes: `revision_required: true, revision_source: patron | review_loop`.
215	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:305:---
216	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:307:## Open Questions
217	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:309:These questions could not be resolved without patron input or empirical data from MVP deployment. Flag for patron review before implementation.
218	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:311:**Q1: Default-on or opt-in?**
219	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:313:The spec recommends opt-in for MVP (`enabled: false` default) to avoid surprises during initial deployment. However, opting-in per guild means the review loop doesn't run in experiments where it would produce the most useful data. Consider making it default-on from the start, with `enabled: false` as the escape hatch for commissions where review is inappropriate (e.g. spec-writing commissions like this one, where there's no build/test to run).
220	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:315:**Q2: Should revision sessions open new drafts or continue in the existing worktree?**
221	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:317:The current design continues in the existing worktree. This means revision builds on what the first attempt produced — which is usually correct (fix what's broken, don't start over). But it also means the revision session can see a messy worktree with uncommitted changes from the first attempt. Does the first attempt's work contaminate the revision? Or is seeing it in context (via `git diff`) actually helpful? No empirical evidence yet.
222	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:319:**Q3: What is the revision session's role?**
223	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:321:Should the revising anima be the same role as the implementing anima (e.g. `artificer`)? Or should the review loop summon a different role with explicit "you are reviewing and fixing prior work" instructions? The current spec defaults to the same role with a modified prompt. A distinct `revisor` role with specialized temperament could perform better. Needs a/b testing once the loop is running.
224	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:323:**Q4: Should the review pass happen before sealing, or is it implicitly "before sealing"?**
225	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:325:The current design places the review pass between the implementation session and the seal step. This means the draft is open during review. If the review pass runs the test suite, the test suite runs inside the worktree before sealing — which is correct. But it also means the worktree is mutable during review (in theory another process could write to it). Is this a problem in practice? Probably not for single-dispatch guilds, but worth noting.
226	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:327:**Q5: LLM-as-judge: when and how?**
227	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:329:The spec defers LLM-as-judge review to future scope, but it's the most valuable future criterion. Key unresolved questions: which model? What's the prompt structure? What's the acceptance threshold (0-10 score? binary pass/fail from the judge)? Who pays for the judge session — is it accounted separately from the commission cost? These need design work before the feature is useful.
228	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:331:**Q6: Should the review loop apply to spec-writing commissions?**
229	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:333:This commission is itself a spec-writing commission. There's no build command to run, no test suite to pass. The only mechanical check that applies is `uncommitted_changes`. Is that sufficient to warrant running the loop? Or should spec-writing commissions (like this one, with no target codex build) opt out of the loop by default? Consider: a charge type hint (`spec` vs. `implementation`) could guide the origination engine to include or exclude the review loop in the initial rig.
230	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:335:---
231	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:337:## Future Evolution
232	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:339:### Phase 2 (Spider-level engine designs)
233	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:340:- `review` clockwork engine contributed by a kit
234	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:341:- `revise` quick engine contributed by the same kit
235	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:342:- Origination engine seeds review graph by default
236	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:343:- Review configuration passed per-rig, not just per-guild
237	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:345:### Phase 3 (Richer review criteria)
238	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:346:- LLM-as-judge `spec_coverage` check
239	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:347:- `type_check` and `lint` checks
240	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:348:- Per-commission review configuration (charge type → review strategy)
241	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:349:- Distinct `revisor` role with specialized temperament
242	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:351:### Phase 4 (Dynamic extension)
243	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:352:- Review engine declares `need: 'revision'` on failure
244	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:353:- Fabricator resolves revision chain dynamically
245	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:354:- Arbitrary retry depth (or patron-configured per-commission)
246	docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:355:- Review loop data feeds Surveyor codex profiles (this codex has a 60% first-try rate → seed richer review graph by default)

docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:1:# The Review Loop — Design Spec
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:3:Status: **Design** (not yet implemented)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:5:> **Not a traditional apparatus.** The review loop does not have a `start()`/`stop()` lifecycle or a persistent runtime API. It is a composition pattern — a pair of engine designs and a rig structure — within the rigging system. This document specifies the design as implemented in the Spider.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:7:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:9:## Purpose
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:11:The review loop moves quality assurance inside the rig. Instead of dispatching a commission once and surfacing the result to the patron regardless of quality, the rig runs an implementation pass, evaluates the result against concrete criteria, and — if the criteria are not met — runs a revision pass. The patron receives work only after it has cleared at least one automated review gate, or after the loop has exhausted its retry budget.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:13:This is not a general-purpose test harness. The review loop does one thing: catch the most common and cheapest-to-detect failure modes before they become patron problems.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:15:**What the review loop is not:**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:16:- A replacement for spec quality. A bad spec produces bad work; the review loop helps only when the anima had the information to succeed but failed in execution.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:17:- A Clockworks-dependent system. The loop runs entirely within the rigging pipeline using existing apparatus.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:18:- A complete quality gate. The MVP catches mechanical failures; richer review criteria are future scope.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:20:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:22:## Empirical Motivation
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:24:Commission log X013 (`experiments/data/commission-log.yaml`) through 2026-04-02 shows the following outcome distribution across patron-tracked commissions with known outcomes:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:26:| Outcome | Count | Notes |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:27:|---------|-------|-------|
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:28:| success | 7 | Includes 1 with revision_required=true (partial attribution issue) |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:29:| partial | 2 | Required follow-up commissions |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:30:| abandoned | 3 | Two were test/infra noise; one was execution_error |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:31:| cancelled | 1 | Process failure, not work failure |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:33:Of the real work failures, the two most common causes were:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:34:1. **Uncommitted changes** — anima produced correct work but did not commit before session end. Mechanically detectable.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:35:2. **Partial execution** — anima completed some of the spec but missed a subsystem (e.g. missed a test file, broke a build). Partially detectable via build/test runs.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:37:Both are catchable with cheap, mechanical review criteria. Neither requires an LLM judge. This is the MVP's target.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:39:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:41:## Design Decision: Where Does the Loop Live?
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:43:Three candidate locations were considered:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:45:### Option B: Review engine in every rig (full design)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:47:The Spider seeds every rig with an `implement → review → [revise → review]*N` chain by default. The review engine is a clockwork engine; the revise engine is a quick engine. Both are standard engine designs contributed by a kit.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:49:**Pros:** Architecturally clean. Composes naturally with Spider's traversal. Reusable engine designs. Composes naturally with the Spider — the rig handles iteration natively.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:51:**Cons:** Requires the Spider. Not implementable until the rigging system exists.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:53:### Option C: Rig pattern via origination engine
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:55:The origination engine seeds rigs with review chains by default. Superficially similar to Option B, but the decision of whether to include a review loop is made at origination time, not by a default rig structure.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:57:**Pros:** Gives origination agency over review strategy (some work may not need review; some may need richer review).
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:59:**Cons:** Complicates origination. Review is almost always appropriate; making it opt-in inverts the sensible default.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:61:### Decision
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:63:**Option B (review engines in the rig) is the chosen design.**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:65:The Spider seeds every rig with an `implement → review → revise → seal` chain. The review engine is a clockwork engine that runs mechanical checks and a reviewer session; the revise engine is a quick engine. Both are standard engine designs contributed by the Spider's support kit. The rig pattern (Option C) governs per-commission review configuration as a future enhancement.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:67:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:69:## Review Engines in the Rig
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:71:### Engine Designs
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:73:#### `review` engine (clockwork)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:75:**Design:**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:76:```typescript
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:77:{
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:78:  id: 'review',
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:79:  kind: 'clockwork',
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:80:  inputs: ['writId', 'worktreePath', 'attempt'],
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:81:  outputs: ['reviewResult'],
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:82:  config: {
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:83:    checks: ['uncommitted_changes', 'build', 'test'],
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:84:    buildCommand: string | undefined,
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:85:    testCommand: string | undefined,
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:86:  }
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:87:}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:88:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:90:The review engine runs the same three checks as the MVP. It writes a `ReviewResult` to its yield. It does not branch — it always completes, passing the result downstream.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:92:The downstream engine (either a `seal` engine or a `revise` engine) reads `reviewResult.passed` to decide what to do. The Spider sees a completed engine regardless of outcome; the branching logic lives in the rig structure (see Rig Pattern below).
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:94:#### `revise` engine (quick)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:96:**Design:**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:97:```typescript
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:98:{
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:99:  id: 'revise',
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:100:  kind: 'quick',
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:101:  inputs: ['writId', 'worktreePath', 'reviewResult', 'attempt'],
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:102:  outputs: ['sessionResult'],
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:103:  role: 'artificer',
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:104:}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:105:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:107:The revise engine assembles the revision prompt (same template as MVP) and launches an anima session. The session runs in the existing worktree — it does not open a new draft.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:109:### Rig Pattern
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:111:The default rig for a commission with review enabled:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:113:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:114:                ┌──────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:115:                │  implement   │  (quick engine: artificer)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:116:                │    engine    │
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:117:                └──────┬───────┘
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:118:                       │ yield: sessionResult
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:119:                       ▼
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:120:                ┌──────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:121:                │    review    │  (clockwork engine)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:122:                │   engine 1  │
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:123:                └──────┬───────┘
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:124:                       │ yield: reviewResult
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:125:          ┌────────────┴────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:126:          │ passed                  │ failed (attempt < maxRetries)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:127:          ▼                         ▼
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:128:   ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:129:   │    seal     │         │     revise       │  (quick engine: artificer)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:130:   │   engine    │         │     engine 1     │
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:131:   └─────────────┘         └────────┬─────────┘
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:132:                                    │ yield: sessionResult
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:133:                                    ▼
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:134:                           ┌──────────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:135:                           │     review       │  (clockwork engine)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:136:                           │    engine 2      │
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:137:                           └────────┬─────────┘
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:138:                                    │ yield: reviewResult
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:139:                       ┌────────────┴────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:140:                       │ passed                  │ failed
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:141:                       ▼                         ▼
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:142:                ┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────────┐
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:143:                │    seal     │         │    escalate      │  (clockwork engine)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:144:                │   engine    │         │    engine        │
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:145:                └─────────────┘         └──────────────────┘
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:146:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:148:The Spider traverses this graph naturally. Each engine completes and propagates its yield; downstream engines activate when their upstream is complete. The conditional branching (pass → seal, fail → revise) is expressed in the rig structure, not in Spider logic — the Spider just runs whatever is ready.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:150:**Seeding the rig:** The origination engine produces this graph when it seeds the rig. For `maxRetries=2`, the origination engine seeds a fixed graph (not dynamically extended). If the guild wants `maxRetries=0` (no review loop), origination seeds the simple `implement → seal` graph.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:152:**Dynamic extension (future):** A more sophisticated design would have the review engine declare a `need: 'revision'` when it fails, and the Fabricator would resolve and graft the next revise+review pair. This avoids pre-seeding the full graph and enables arbitrary retry depths. This is Future scope — the fixed graph is sufficient for MVP and avoids Spider complexity in the initial rigging implementation.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:154:### Spider Integration
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:156:The Spider needs no changes to support the review loop. It already:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:157:- Traverses all engines whose upstream is complete
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:158:- Routes ready engines to the Executor
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:159:- Handles both clockwork and quick engine kinds
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:161:The review loop is just a graph shape that Spider happens to traverse. The `escalate` clockwork engine signals the Clerk with a `failed` transition; the `seal` clockwork engine signals completion. The Spider itself is agnostic.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:163:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:165:## Review Criteria Reference
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:167:### MVP Criteria (Mechanical)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:169:| Check | Description | Detection Method | Cost |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:170:|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:171:| `uncommitted_changes` | All work is committed | `git status --porcelain` | < 100ms |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:172:| `build` | Build command exits cleanly | Run configured build command | Varies |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:173:| `test` | Test suite passes | Run configured test command | Varies |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:175:The `uncommitted_changes` check is always enabled. Build and test checks are opt-in via guild configuration.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:177:### Future Criteria (Judgment-Required)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:179:These are not in scope for MVP but are the natural next layer:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:181:| Check | Description | Detection Method | Cost |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:182:|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:183:| `spec_coverage` | Diff addresses spec requirements | LLM-as-judge pass on (spec, diff) | Medium |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:184:| `no_regressions` | No tests were deleted or disabled | Diff analysis | Low |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:185:| `type_check` | TypeScript compilation passes | `tsc --noEmit` | Varies |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:186:| `lint` | Linter passes | Run configured lint command | Varies |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:188:The LLM-as-judge `spec_coverage` check is the most valuable future criterion — it catches the "anima only addressed part of the spec" failure mode that mechanical checks miss. It requires a separate quick engine with access to the writ body and the diff, and a structured prompt asking whether the diff achieves the spec's stated goals.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:190:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:192:## Artifact Schema
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:194:Every review pass writes an artifact. Artifacts live in the commission data directory alongside the existing artifacts written by the Laboratory.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:196:### Location
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:198:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:199:experiments/data/commissions/<writ-id>/
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:200:  commission.md          (existing — writ body)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:201:  review.md              (existing template — patron review slot)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:202:  review-loop/
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:203:    attempt-1/
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:204:      review.md          (ReviewResult as structured markdown)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:205:      git-status.txt     (git status output)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:206:      git-diff.txt       (git diff HEAD output)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:207:    attempt-2/
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:208:      review.md
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:209:      git-status.txt
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:210:      git-diff.txt
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:211:    escalation.md        (if loop exhausted; patron-facing summary)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:212:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:214:The review engine writes these artifacts via the Stacks or directly to the commission data directory.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:216:### `review.md` Schema
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:218:```markdown
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:219:# Review — Attempt {N}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:221:**Writ:** {writId}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:222:**Timestamp:** {ISO 8601}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:223:**Result:** PASSED | FAILED
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:225:## Checks
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:227:| Check | Result | Duration |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:228:|-------|--------|----------|
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:229:| uncommitted_changes | ✓ PASS / ✗ FAIL | {ms}ms |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:230:| build | ✓ PASS / ✗ FAIL | {ms}ms |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:231:| test | ✓ PASS / ✗ FAIL | {ms}ms |
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:233:## Failures
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:235:{for each failure}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:236:### {check}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:237:{message}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:239:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:240:{detail}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:241:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:242:{end for}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:243:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:245:### `escalation.md` Schema
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:247:```markdown
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:248:# Review Loop Escalated
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:250:**Writ:** {writId}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:251:**Title:** {writ.title}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:252:**Attempts:** {N}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:253:**Timestamp:** {ISO 8601}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:255:The review loop exhausted its retry budget ({maxRetries} retries) without
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:256:achieving a passing review. The draft has been abandoned.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:258:## Summary of Failures
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:260:{for each attempt}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:261:### Attempt {N}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:262:{list of failed checks with messages}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:263:{end for}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:265:## Recommended Actions
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:267:- Inspect the worktree state preserved in the draft artifacts
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:268:- Review the git-diff.txt files in each attempt directory
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:269:- Revise the spec to address the observed failure mode before re-dispatching
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:270:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:272:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:274:## Configuration
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:276:Review configuration lives in `guild.json`:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:278:```json
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:279:{
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:280:  "review": {
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:281:    "enabled": true,
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:282:    "maxRetries": 2,
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:283:    "buildCommand": "pnpm build",
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:284:    "testCommand": "pnpm test"
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:285:  }
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:286:}
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:287:```
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:289:All fields are optional. `enabled` defaults to `false` (opt-in). The intent is to make it default-on once the loop has been validated in practice. This configuration is consumed by the origination engine to decide whether to seed the review graph and what configuration to pass to the review engine.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:291:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:293:## Observability
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:295:The review loop is itself experiment data. Every iteration produces artifacts that the Laboratory can capture and analyze:
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:297:1. **Review artifacts** (`review-loop/attempt-N/`) — structured pass/fail evidence for each check. Enables quantitative analysis: which checks catch what failure modes? How often does the second attempt pass where the first failed?
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:299:2. **Session records** — revision sessions are recorded in the Animator's `sessions` book with `metadata.trigger: 'review-revision'` and `metadata.attempt: N`. Enables cost accounting: how much does the review loop add per commission?
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:301:3. **Writ resolution field** — when the loop escalates, the writ resolution includes the retry count. The commission log's `failure_mode` can be set to `review_exhausted` to distinguish review-loop failures from first-try failures.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:303:4. **Commission log** — the `revision_required` field will more accurately reflect anima-driven revisions vs. patron-driven revisions once the review loop is active. The distinction becomes: `revision_required: true, revision_source: patron | review_loop`.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:305:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:307:## Open Questions
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:309:These questions could not be resolved without patron input or empirical data from MVP deployment. Flag for patron review before implementation.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:311:**Q1: Default-on or opt-in?**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:313:The spec recommends opt-in for MVP (`enabled: false` default) to avoid surprises during initial deployment. However, opting-in per guild means the review loop doesn't run in experiments where it would produce the most useful data. Consider making it default-on from the start, with `enabled: false` as the escape hatch for commissions where review is inappropriate (e.g. spec-writing commissions like this one, where there's no build/test to run).
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:315:**Q2: Should revision sessions open new drafts or continue in the existing worktree?**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:317:The current design continues in the existing worktree. This means revision builds on what the first attempt produced — which is usually correct (fix what's broken, don't start over). But it also means the revision session can see a messy worktree with uncommitted changes from the first attempt. Does the first attempt's work contaminate the revision? Or is seeing it in context (via `git diff`) actually helpful? No empirical evidence yet.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:319:**Q3: What is the revision session's role?**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:321:Should the revising anima be the same role as the implementing anima (e.g. `artificer`)? Or should the review loop summon a different role with explicit "you are reviewing and fixing prior work" instructions? The current spec defaults to the same role with a modified prompt. A distinct `revisor` role with specialized temperament could perform better. Needs a/b testing once the loop is running.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:323:**Q4: Should the review pass happen before sealing, or is it implicitly "before sealing"?**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:325:The current design places the review pass between the implementation session and the seal step. This means the draft is open during review. If the review pass runs the test suite, the test suite runs inside the worktree before sealing — which is correct. But it also means the worktree is mutable during review (in theory another process could write to it). Is this a problem in practice? Probably not for single-dispatch guilds, but worth noting.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:327:**Q5: LLM-as-judge: when and how?**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:329:The spec defers LLM-as-judge review to future scope, but it's the most valuable future criterion. Key unresolved questions: which model? What's the prompt structure? What's the acceptance threshold (0-10 score? binary pass/fail from the judge)? Who pays for the judge session — is it accounted separately from the commission cost? These need design work before the feature is useful.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:331:**Q6: Should the review loop apply to spec-writing commissions?**
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:333:This commission is itself a spec-writing commission. There's no build command to run, no test suite to pass. The only mechanical check that applies is `uncommitted_changes`. Is that sufficient to warrant running the loop? Or should spec-writing commissions (like this one, with no target codex build) opt out of the loop by default? Consider: a charge type hint (`spec` vs. `implementation`) could guide the origination engine to include or exclude the review loop in the initial rig.
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:335:---
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:337:## Future Evolution
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:339:### Phase 2 (Spider-level engine designs)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:340:- `review` clockwork engine contributed by a kit
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:341:- `revise` quick engine contributed by the same kit
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:342:- Origination engine seeds review graph by default
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:343:- Review configuration passed per-rig, not just per-guild
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:345:### Phase 3 (Richer review criteria)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:346:- LLM-as-judge `spec_coverage` check
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:347:- `type_check` and `lint` checks
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:348:- Per-commission review configuration (charge type → review strategy)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:349:- Distinct `revisor` role with specialized temperament
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:351:### Phase 4 (Dynamic extension)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:352:- Review engine declares `need: 'revision'` on failure
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:353:- Fabricator resolves revision chain dynamically
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:354:- Arbitrary retry depth (or patron-configured per-commission)
docs/architecture/apparatus/review-loop.md:355:- Review loop data feeds Surveyor codex profiles (this codex has a 60% first-try rate → seed richer review graph by default)

docs/architecture/index.md:75:When The Clerk signals a writ is ready, **The Spider** spawns a rig and begins driving it: traversing active engines, dispatching those whose upstream work is complete, and extending the rig by querying **The Fabricator** for engine chains that satisfy declared needs. **The Executor** runs each engine — clockwork engines run their code directly; quick engines launch an anima session.
docs/architecture/index.md:155:**`nexus.db`** — the SQLite database owned by The Stacks. All guild state that needs to survive process restarts lives here: anima records, writ history, session records, event and dispatch logs.
docs/architecture/index.md:404:**Relays** are Clockworks handlers — purpose-built to respond to events via standing orders. A relay exports a standard `relay()` contract that the Clockworks runner calls when a matching event fires. All relays are clockwork (no anima involvement). The built-in **summon relay** is the mechanism that dispatches anima sessions in response to standing orders.
docs/architecture/index.md:463:Every session record captures structured telemetry: wall-clock duration, exit code, token usage (input, output, cache read, cache write), and cost in USD. Callers attach opaque **metadata** — the Animator stores it without interpreting it. The summon relay attaches dispatch context (writ id, anima name, codex); `nsg consult` attaches interactive session context. Downstream queries against metadata use The Stacks' JSON path queries.
docs/guides/building-engines.md:7:Engines are automated processes with no AI involvement. They run deterministic logic in response to events: rolling up completion status, dispatching work, transforming data, enforcing policies. They don't have instruction documents because no anima wields them — they're guild infrastructure.
docs/guides/building-engines.md:51:4. **Throw for errors.** If the handler throws, the Clockworks runner catches the error, records a failed dispatch, and signals `standing-order.failed`.
docs/guides/building-engines.md:210:2. Records a failed dispatch in `event_dispatches` (with the error message)
docs/architecture/apparatus/claude-code.md:293:This would eliminate per-session HTTP server startup for batch operations (e.g., dispatching multiple artificer sessions). The savings are modest — HTTP server start is fast — but it reduces port churn and simplifies cleanup in high-throughput scenarios.
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:111:Anima calls dispatch(...)
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:113:  → MCP engine calls dispatch handler
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:141:- **When to use the tool** — "Always consult the Master Sage before dispatching to artificers"
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:142:- **When NOT to use it** — "Don't dispatch if the commission spec lacks acceptance criteria"
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:143:- **Workflow context** — "After dispatching, record the commission ID in your notes for the handoff"
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:146:- **Interaction with other tools** — "If dispatch returns a conflict, use get-anima to check the anima's current commission before retrying"
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:174:  "repository": "https://github.com/nexus/dispatch",
docs/architecture/kit-components.md:182:There is no `name` field — the **directory name is the tool's identity**. After installation, the directory name (`dispatch/`, `my-relay/`) is the canonical name. During installation from npm, the directory name is derived from the package name (strip scope: `@shardworks/dispatch` → `dispatch`) or specified with `--name`.
docs/architecture/apparatus/spider.md:334:The implement engine wraps the writ body with a commit instruction — each engine owns its own prompt contract rather than relying on `dispatch.sh` to append instructions to the writ body.
docs/architecture/apparatus/scriptorium.md:15:The Scriptorium does **not** know what a codex contains or what work applies to it (that's the Surveyor's domain). It does **not** orchestrate which anima works in which draft (that's the caller's concern — rig engines, dispatch scripts, or direct human invocation). It is pure git infrastructure — repository lifecycle, draft isolation, and branch management.
docs/architecture/apparatus/scriptorium.md:494:  Orchestrator (dispatch script, rig engine, standing order)
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:24:Events are persisted to the Clockworks' own event queue immediately when signaled. They do not carry intent — they carry record. An event says "this occurred"; it does not say "therefore do this." That causal link lives in standing orders. The event and dispatch tables are internal Clockworks operational state — not part of the guild's Books (Register, Ledger, Daybook).
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:40:| `{type}.ready` | A writ transitions to `ready` — available for dispatch (e.g. `mandate.ready`, `task.ready`) |
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:123:The `summon` key is shorthand for invoking the **summon relay** — the stdlib relay that handles anima session dispatch. The Clockworks desugars `summon` orders at dispatch time:
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:129:// What the Clockworks dispatches:
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:133:The `summon` value becomes the `role` param. All other keys pass through as relay params. This means anima dispatch is handled by a regular relay — replaceable, upgradeable, configurable — not baked into the framework.
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:135:The **summon relay** resolves the role to an active anima, binds or synthesizes a writ, manifests the anima, hydrates the prompt template, launches a session, and handles post-session writ lifecycle. See [Dispatch Integration](writs.md#dispatch-integration) for the full sequence.
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:145:**Circuit breaker:** By default, the summon relay will fail a writ after 10 session attempts. This prevents infinite re-dispatch loops when a writ keeps getting interrupted without making progress. Override per standing order with `"maxSessions": 20` or disable with `"maxSessions": 0`.
docs/architecture/clockworks.md:196:The daemon registers the session provider at startup, enabling the summon relay to dispatch anima sessions autonomously.
